text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members merge their code changes into a shared repository frequently. Each time code is submitted, the CI pipeline automatically starts an automated build to compile the program. After the build, it runs a series of tests to check for errors or bugs. The main goal is catching issues early in the development process, before they become bigger problems. If a test fails, developers are notified immediately, allowing them to fix the code quickly. This saves time and improves the overall quality of the software, making CI an essential part of modern development teams.
